seoTitle: Highfields vs Hill Top — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Highfields and Hill Top. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Highfields vs Hill Top: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Highfields and Hill Top.
heading: Highfields vs Hill Top — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Highfields, New South Wales and Hill Top, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Highfields has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Hill Top (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Highfields or Hill Top safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Highfields has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Hill Top scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Highfields scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Hill Top scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Highfields or Hill Top?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Highfields has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
